programme.title: Capítulo 196
programme.desc: Diego is distraught over Roberta and Simon. Mía pretends to be drunk and kisses Miguel. Valeria had a nervous breakdown. Valeria makes Peter quit. Diego admits he's in love with Roberta. Miguel finds out that Mía wasn't really drunk and confronts her. Gastón reacts angrily when Mía ends their relationship. Simon tells the band they sold out on their concert at the Palacio. León wants the Palacio as his campaign spot. Camilo asks Celina out on a date. Gastón taunts Miguel. Alma confronts Valeria over the letter.

programme.title: Capítulo 197
programme.desc: Valeria denies writing the letter. Celina leaves school without permission; Renata tells her parents. Gastón harasses Miguel and threatens him with the information he gathered in Monterrey. Miguel demands Mía tell Gastón that she not dating him. Giovanni and Vico find out that Madariaga might be leaving. Diego begs Roberta for forgiveness and another chance or he won't do the concert; she agrees. Celina's father shows up at school and gives her an ultimatum. Hilda becomes jealous. Pepa thinks Peter is trying to take her job. Gastón calls Franco to meet. Mía tells Gastón the truth about Miguel. Peter tells Mía he sent the letter.

programme.title: Capítulo 198
programme.desc: Roberta asks Madariaga if he's leaving; he says no. Lupita tries to help Celina with her family troubles. León tells Diego he's searching for the RBD members. Peter tells Mía the letter was written by her mom fifteen years ago. Mía decides to move in with Alma. Galia tells Pilar she will go to therapy; and also that Madariaga is leaving after the year. Celina introduces Camilo and her father. The students help give a makeover to Hilario. Roberta decides to move in with Franco.

programme.title: Capítulo 199
programme.desc: No one believes Pilar when she tells them Madariaga is leaving. Alicia goes on a fake date with Hilario. Marcelino convinces Peter that he's fired. Mía tells her dad she ended it with Gastón. León approaches Alma about RBD. Franco gives Miguel a check for his tuition next year. Gastón reveals Miguel's revenge to Mía. Mía asks Miguel about his dad's death. Diego confronts Roberta about their love.

programme.title: Capítulo 200
programme.desc: Diego tells Roberta he's ready to confront his dad about her and the band. Gastón hears Celina throwing up. Tomás feels like his life is no longer enjoyable. Roberta decides to stay with Simon and not forgive Diego. Franco and Alma fight over Peter. Lupita tells Mía that she won't be going to Orlando. José and the others decide to hire an escort for Alicia.

programme.title: Capítulo 201
programme.desc: It's revealed that Celina has bulimia. Roberta beats up Pilar for saying Madariaga is leaving. Madariaga tells the kids he's leaving. The girls find an escort. Diego, Giovanni, and Tomás get Simon's number and find out he's married. Nico convinces Miguel to show Gastón he is on good terms with Franco. Pilar tells everyone Madariaga was pushed out. The kids go on strike for Madariaga. Alma tries to gather information on Simon. Gandia expels the scholarship students. Renata interrupts Madariaga's meeting to bring him back to the school.

programme.title: Capítulo 202
programme.desc: Mayra scolds the students who walked out of the protest. Madariaga offers his resignation in exchange for the kids not to be expelled. Diego mentions Simon's wife in front of him and Roberta. Madariaga's project is denied. León learns that the members of RBD go to Elite Way School. Giovanni and Tomás find Simon's address. After León's visit, Gandia awkwardly interrogates Diego. Diego gets Roberta to meeting Simon's wife.

programme.title: Capítulo 203
programme.desc: Franco tells Mía that Miguel refuses to accept the house or stay in Mexico. Hilda sees Alicia with Popeye. Alma and Franco once again fight over Peter. Yuli tells Nico she's not going on the family vacation. Mía asks Gastón to tell her what he knows, later he gives her an envelope with evidence against Miguel. Pilar tells Roberta that Diego will give up RBD names. Alma and Diego decide to work together to expose Simon; she sees Simon with his baby. Madariaga rather have the kids angry at him than tell the truth. The kids literally turn their back on Madariaga. Gandia thinks Hilda ordered the escort. Mía reflects on her precious moments with Miguel.

programme.title: Capítulo 204
programme.desc: Gandia calls out the RBD members for expulsion. Mía tells Franco Miguel's dad died because of him and Miguel came here for revenge. Diego's plans to help the band are revealed. Franco tells Mía that Carlo was responsible for Miguel's dad financial ruin and subsequent death. Simon tells Roberta he has a son. Franco grants Gastón permission to protect Mía from Miguel. Gastón threatens Miguel. Celina has a bulimic scare. Carlo returns to Mexico. Mía confronts Miguel about his revenge; the most explosive scene of their relationship.

programme.title: Episode #5.10
programme.desc: The victim's name was Dale Harrell. He was married for ten years and was soon to be a partner at his company as a successful mechanical engineer. He made 83, 000 a year and provided a safe home for his wife and three children in Gilbert, Arizona. At approximately 2;36 AM on January 14, 2009, his wife of ten years, Marissa DeVault, put a hammer in his husband's head five times while he was fast asleep, only hours after having sex. Her first 911 calls to the Gilbert Police Department claimed abuse and rape. The ensuing investigation revealed a story known only to a few and a life kept secret from a husband. There was a roommate with a gun. There was a secret life of a millionaire lover. And, from her days as an exotic dancer, Marissa attempted to hire her former lover and bouncer of a strip club. The killer was not a visitor to the club and she was not a bartender or server. Instead, she could be found on the stage as an entertainer. How did all these characters come together to result in the death of Dale Harrell? Where did the gun come from and how was it instrumental in revealing the truths of a killer? Who was her "Sugar Daddy" and was he an accomplice? Why was the hammer used to kill instead of a gun when the gun was only mere feet of the woman accused. It is a story filled with twists, turns, characters and lust and, in the end, a victim lay dead. His name was Dale Harrell.

programme.title: La peli de Frankie
programme.desc: Frankie searches among his neighbors for inspiration for the characters in his new movie. Additionally, Roco and Alejandra find out that Morris won a scholarship.

programme.title: La jubilación de Arturo y Chabelo
programme.desc: Arturo le cuenta a su familia que por fin podrá jubilarse y tendrán un dinero extra. Mientras tanto, los demás vecinos se reúnen para ver En familia con Chabelo.

programme.title: La estética de Lorena
programme.desc: Lorena tiene dificultades económicas y por eso decide abrir una estética que termina arruinando el cabello de sus clientes.

programme.title: Diva en el edificio
programme.desc: Los vecinos compiten en una trivia de televisión para que Lucía Méndez pase una tarde con ellos. Además, Silvia y Luis descubren que posiblemente esperen otro hijo.

programme.title: El sueño de Rocko
programme.desc: Rocko sueña con tener un grupo de rock llamado Los Moridos y empieza reunir a otros integrantes. Mientras, Jorjais decide mejorar su carrito para incrementar sus propinas.

programme.title: Basura en la escalera
programme.desc: Uno de los vecinos deja su basura en las escaleras, por lo que Luisito quiere encontrar al culpable. Además, Silvia hace de todo para ayudar a su sobrina a buscar empleo.

programme.title: La reina del Pik Pok
programme.desc: Erika Buenfil acude a la colonia para encontrar a nuevos talentos, pero termina conociendo a los vecinos. Además, don Hipólito tiene un nuevo trabajo para Germán.

programme.title: Cumpleaños de Luisito Junior
programme.desc: Es el cumpleaños de Luisito Jr. y Silvia se dispone a realizar una fiesta en privado. Sin embargo, los vecinos descubren el festejo y arman una celebración en grande.

programme.title: Bonita familia
programme.desc: Pedro pierde a Rambo en el póker con El Pechugo y Cris intenta recuperarlo. La familia Rivers recibe una mala noticia que tiene que ver con Don Lorenzo.

programme.title: Elote el guajolote
programme.desc: Vanesa recibe la visita de un inspector de seguridad en su clínica para cerciorarse de que todo esté en orden. Además, los vecinos aconsejan a Germán para conquistar a Virginia.

programme.title: Nos cayeron los chahuiztles
programme.desc: Casimiro Pérez, el hermano de Magda llega de visita. Además, la madrastra de Lorena le trae una noticia que cambiará su situación económica.

programme.title: Adopción
programme.desc: Cris shows Pedro the adoption application but they will receive a visit from the social worker. Germán, on the other hand, will be pruning the trees outside the building, without imagining the problems that this would bring.

programme.title: Buzón de quejas
programme.desc: Los problemas en el edificio revivieron un buzón de quejas que ya estaba olvidado, pero este buzón traerá problemas a Luis y Silvia. Por otro lado Morris esta a punto de reprobar el año.

programme.title: Can guash
programme.desc: Pedro se aterra por la llegada de su suegro pues tiene miedo de que no se lleven bien, además Germán y Vanessa emprenden un nuevo negocio.

programme.title: Oficinas de gobierno
programme.desc: Los vecinos aprovechan la apertura de unas nuevas oficinas de gobierno para emprender sus negocios y obtener algo de dinero, pero cuando Luis los descubre, intentará correrlos de la entrada del edificio.

programme.title: Llegada de Martina
programme.desc: Cris y Pablo están esperando a su hija Martina y, nerviosos por conocerla, preparan todo para recibirla. Por otro lado, Liz y Vanesa abren su tienda de ropa virtual y logran hacer un trato con Don Arturo.

programme.title: El gorrión de la canción
programme.desc: Dulce arrives at the building looking for the author of some songs to compose a new single for her. Also, Luis and Silvia meet the parents of one of their son's classmates.

programme.title: Alejandra godín
programme.desc: Arturo llega con la noticia de que Alejandra será una nueva empleada en la empresa donde trabaja. Por otro lado, una pequeña apuesta entre Germán y Birote crece sin control.

programme.title: Subasta
programme.desc: Magdalena se da cuenta que puede sacarle dinero a los objetos viejos de Arturo y se da a la tarea de hacer una subasta, carísima por cierto.

programme.title: Frankie publicista
programme.desc: Vanesa desesperada por salir de sus deudas, busca la ayuda de Frankie para diseñar la publicidad de su negocio, pero al final se da cuenta que el de las ideas realmente era otro.

programme.title: Sortilegio
programme.desc: Alejandro returns home after a terrible accident to discover that he is married to Maria Jose, a poor, innocent girl who was deceived by Alejandro's brother Bruno. Bruno used Alejandro's name to marry Maria Jose, soon after he sent to kill his brother in hopes that Maria Jose would inherit Alejandro's fortune. But when Maria Jose arrives at the mansion she realizes that Bruno lied to her and used her. She is also scared when Alejandro is found alive and returns home. Doctors tell Alejandro that he suffers from memory loss but he is convinced he never met Maria Jose before and goes in search of the truth, but during this time and Maria Jose actually fall in love, while Bruno and Maura (Alejandro's ex-girlfriend) plot to break them up. Meanwhile, the brothers' mother Victoria has fallen in love with Fernando--Alejandro's best friend, so closer to his age than hers.

programme.title: Bad Vibrations
programme.desc: The end of the season is near and the crews scramble to finish strong.

programme.title: MeatEater - The Northern Rockies: British Columbia Grizzly Part 2
programme.desc: Steve and his buddy Ryan Callaghan, a British Columbia guide, hike into the steep backcountry of northern British Columbia in search of grizzlies and black bears. Among the beutiful alpine vista, this adventure includes some intense and up-close bear action ‚Äì almost too close.

programme.title: MeatEater - Opening Day: Wisconsin Whitetail Deer Part 1
programme.desc: Steven Rinella and his buddy Doug Duren give comedians Joe Rogan and Bryan Callen their first taste.

programme.title: MeatEater - Opening Day: Wisconsin Whitetail Deer Part 2
programme.desc: Steven Rinella and his buddy Doug Duren hunt with Joe Rogan.

programme.title: MeatEater - Spring Break: Florida Osceola Turkey
programme.desc: Steven Rinella and his friend and wild turkey specialist, Robert Abernathy, head to southern Florida to hunt.

programme.title: MeatEater - Boone and Bugles: Kentucky Bull Elk
programme.desc: Steven Rinella is invited by the Rocky Mountain Elk Foundation for a special opportunity to hunt elk during the rut in the mountainous coal country of southeastern Kentucky, a place where his hero Daniel Boone once roamed. This is Steve's first eastern elk hunt, and he quickly realizes that the rules of the game here are different. If he manages to track one down, he'll prepare what was supposedly Boone's favorite meal: elk liver.

programme.title: MeatEater - Cold Water: Alaskan Seafood Cooking Special
programme.desc: Join Steve on this Cooking Special as he dives into the best of Alaskan seafood, featuring shrimp, crab, fish, and clams. Learn how to make easy, delicious seafood recipes that showcase the fresh flavors of the sea. Whether you're a seasoned cook or a beginner, Steve's simple steps will help you prepare mouthwatering dishes that capture the essence of Alaskan waters.

programme.title: MeatEater - Big Buck Dreams: Colorado Mule Deer
programme.desc: Steve's annual quest for a giant mule deer buck continues this year in southwest Colorado. Here, Steve fills us in on his favorite critter, the mule deer. As he hunts will share his vast knowledge about these special western game animals including how to hunt, butcher, and most importantly how cook them-if he can put his tag on one.

programme.title: MeatEater - Surf N Turk: Wisconsin Turkey and Carp
programme.desc: Steven Rinella scratches his spring turkey itch this year as he visits Southeast Wisconsin to call in some big eastern Toms with his buddy Jerod Fink. The guys also plan to do some bow fishing after dark so they can enjoy a real Midwest turkey and fish fry.

programme.title: MeatEater - The Best Start - Wild Game Breakfast Cooking Special
programme.desc: Breakfast is the most important meal of the day, but is often overlooked by wild game cooks at home. Following a series of successful hunts across North America this past fall, Steve demonstrates how to cook a variety of breakfast classics using wild game ingredients‚Äîincluding black bear bacon, which Steve has never tried before.

programme.title: MeatEater - Gila Monster: New Mexico Bull Elk
programme.desc: After four years of waiting, Steven Rinella draws a coveted, limited-entry public land bull elk tag for New Mexico's Gila National Forest. Steve heads into the backcountry on a solo backpack hunt where his biggest challenge is the vast expanse of hills covered in thick timber. This demanding hunt requires lots of glassing and even more hiking. Here, even just one missed opportunity could result in an empty freezer.

programme.title: MeatEater - Hearts of Darkness: Ticker Cooking Special
programme.desc: While its considered a long-standing hunting tradition to eat the heart of your first kill, the heart.

programme.title: MeatEater - Bear With Seal: Alaska Bear Hunt Part 1
programme.desc: Steven Rinella will take Navy SEAL Rorke Denver on his first hunt deep into the Alaskan backcountry to search for black bears in the mighty Alaska Range. In the remote wilderness, two friends will look into the differences and similarities between the hunter and the warrior. The bears are out but Rorke and Steve will have to work hard to put a tag on one.

programme.title: MeatEater - Bear With Seal: Alaska Bear Hunt Part 2
programme.desc: Steven Rinella will take Navy SEAL Rorke Denver on his first hunt deep into the Alaskan backcountry to search for black bears in the mighty Alaska Range. In the remote wilderness, two friends will look into the differences and similarities between the hunter and the warrior. The bears are out but Rorke and Steve will have to work hard to put a tag on one.

programme.title: MeatEater - Yukon Giants: Northern Alaska Moose Part 1
programme.desc: Dropped off by bushplane on a remote lakeside in the wilderness of Alaska's Brooks Range, Steven Rinella and wildlife biologist Brandt Meixell have ten days of hunting before the plane returns. Big-antlered Yukon moose are few and far between but hard work pays off for hunters who stick with it until the bitter end.

programme.title: MeatEater - Yukon Giants: Northern Alaska Moose Part 2
programme.desc: Dropped off by bushplane on a remote lakeside in the wilderness of Alaskas Brooks Range.

programme.title: MeatEater - Deer the Hard Way: Prince of Wales Island (Joe Rogan and Bryan Callen)
programme.desc: Steven Rinella has hunted this area before, but he feels he's just scratched the surface exploring the surrounding country. The top of the mountain is always calling Steve's name, and for this serious Alaskan backpack hunt he's bringing along comedians Joe Rogan and Bryan Callen. Cold, wet weather and tough hunting for wily Sitka deer make this a southeast Alaskan adventure these hunters will never forget.

programme.title: Pod Save America (LIVE(ish) from the Obama Presidential Center)
programme.desc: Jon, Lovett, Tommy, and Dan record from Chicago after the dedication ceremony of the Obama Presidential Center, reacting to Barack and Michelle Obama's speeches, what it felt like to walk through the museum for the first time, and what lessons Democrats in the Trump era can take from Obama. Then, they react to the emerging details of Donald Trump's MOU with Iran, which he signed at the Palace of Versailles, JD Vance's efforts to seem like a normal person during his book tour, and why the algae in the Reflecting Pool is such an apt metaphor for Trump's presidency.

programme.title: Offline with Jon Favreau (Has The Left Finally Figured Out the Internet?)
programme.desc: Ever heard a popular TikToker wax poetic about who they’re voting for? They might be getting paid…and not disclosing by whom. Kyle Tharp, author of the Chaotic Era newsletter, joins Offline to discuss the emergence of pay-for-play political influencers and whether Democrats have made any progress in figuring out the internet. He and Jon talk about the decline of Ben Shapiro and the Daily Wire, the new success of legacy media on TikTok, and the intense grassroots anger around data centers.

programme.title: This F*cking Guy (Karoline Leavitt: Everything You Didn't Know About Her Sh*tty Past)
programme.desc: In our 38th episode of This F*cking Guy, Erin and Alyssa dive deep into the past of the MAGA mouthpiece, Karoline Leavitt. From “growing up in a small business family” to her college broadcasting days, to being the ultimate tradwife hypocrite, to shapeshifting into an obsequious Trump a** kisser, this may be one of our most irritating guys yet!

programme.title: Pod Save America (80-Year-Old Man Loses War)
programme.desc: Trump ends his war against Iran without accomplishing any of the goals he set at the war's onset. Jon, Tommy, and Lovett discuss how real the deal to end the war actually is, how it's playing out in MAGA world, and its implications for the economy and the midterms. Then, they react to the corruption at the UFC fight at the White House and Gov. Gavin Newsom's announcement that he and his wife are being targeted by Trump's Justice Department. Finally, Sen. Mark Warner talks to Tommy about the situation in Iran and Trump's nominee for Director of National Intelligence.

programme.title: Pod Save America (LIVE(ish) from the Obama Presidential Center)
programme.desc: Jon, Lovett, Tommy, and Dan record from Chicago after the dedication ceremony of the Obama Presidential Center, reacting to Barack and Michelle Obama's speeches, what it felt like to walk through the museum for the first time, and what lessons Democrats in the Trump era can take from Obama. Then, they react to the emerging details of Donald Trump's MOU with Iran, which he signed at the Palace of Versailles, JD Vance's efforts to seem like a normal person during his book tour, and why the algae in the Reflecting Pool is such an apt metaphor for Trump's presidency.

programme.title: Assembly Required with Stacey Abrams (How Billionaires Are Destroying the Media (with Joy Reid))
programme.desc: The media landscape in the US continues to undergo drastic transformations, with billionaire ownership and corporate consolidation leading to mass layoffs and content censorship. This week, Stacey is joined by Joy Reid, host of the Joy Reid Show and the first black woman to host a primetime program on cable news. They talk about the layoffs at the Washington Post, why she transitioned into independent media, what is lost when Black journalists are no longer at the table, and what people can do to inform themselves in an era where billionaires with agendas control the news.

programme.title: Pod Save America (Graham Platner Isn't Backing Down)
programme.desc: Graham Platner, a Democratic candidate running for Senate in Maine, stops by the studio to talk with Jon about Trump’s impending conflict with Iran, the future of Medicare for All, and what community organizing in rural Maine taught him about building political power in our polarized era. The two discuss new polls showing Platner leading Janet Mills in the Democratic Senate primary, how his tattoo controversy has resonated with Maine voters, and what he wants to change about the Democratic Party to rebuild a winning, working-class coalition.

programme.title: Don't Count Your Tomatoes Before They're Picked
programme.desc: For the first time, Oliver has a successful crop that needs picking. His tomatoes have grown beautifully. But matters at the phone company continue divide his precious time, so Oliver looks for an interested buyer to take the company off his hands.

programme.title: A Kind Word For The President
programme.desc: Being president of the Hooterville Phone Company has been a rude awakening for Oliver. The switchboard operator is now calling him at five in the morning! After a bevy of new complaints from unhappy customers, Oliver has just about had enough.

programme.title: Eb Elopes
programme.desc: One might think that when Eb takes a vacation things around the farm might go more smoothly. But when Eb elopes, leaving his cousin Walter to take his place, it turns out to be a horrible idea. Walter barely knows a weed from a hole in the ground!

programme.title: The Thing
programme.desc: Lisa is reminded that she stored a birthday present for Oliver years ago: a genuine Stavinsky. But what is it? It's a modern art sculpture that is two parts plumbing pipes, one part tire chains, and one part birdbath. Oliver calls it "The Thing."

programme.title: Das Lumpen
programme.desc: The continually evolving story of how Lisa (Eva Gabor) and Oliver (Oliver) fell in love gets yet another version in this delightful episode. But one thing is for sure: Lisa and Oliver are just as in love today as the day when he fell out of the sky.

programme.title: Jealousy, English Style
programme.desc: Green Acres is greener than ever, with jealousy. When Oliver (Eddie Albert) hires a handsome, young, intelligent Brit to take over his farm work while he is away, it doesn't take long for the newcomer to make a strong impression on Lisa (Eva Gabor).

programme.title: Haney's New Image
programme.desc: Mr. Haney is up to his money-making schemes again. When a government representative tells Mr. Haney that the state is looking to purchase the Douglas farm for a new highway, Mr. Haney arranges to buy back the farm from the Douglases at a steal.

programme.title: Alf And Ralph Break Up
programme.desc: Ralph, the lady carpenter, runs away from home and stays with the Douglases after having a fight with her brother, Alf. While Oliver may never get his bedroom finished, he gains a maid as Ralph takes over as the Douglases' live-in housekeeper.

programme.title: No Trespassing
programme.desc: Picking apples while on a picnic, Oliver runs into the feisty Ira Hatch, the owner of the apple orchard. However, once back in town, Mr. Drucker tells Oliver that Hatch has been dead for years. An incredulous Oliver agrees to see a psychologist.

programme.title: Eb Returns
programme.desc: After his elopement and cross-country honeymoon, Eb returns to Hooterville. But his new wife is nowhere to be found! After sending postcards from all across the country, Eb has to confess that his fianc� stood him up at the alter on their big day.

programme.title: Not Guilty
programme.desc: Someone has stolen $300 from Mr. Drucker's general store, and it looks like Eb is the culprit. How else could he have bought himself a new car that cost, you guessed it, exactly $300? But that is just the first twist of the many turns of this story.

programme.title: How To Succeed In TV Without Really Trying
programme.desc: Lisa awakes, saying she's dreamt that she must invest in copper. When Oliver turns that idea down, she seizes on the opportunity to invest in a ten-year-old genius's electric company. Twenty-eight dollars buys her a third of the business.

programme.title: Arnold, Boy Hero
programme.desc: Arnold, the Ziffels' television-loving pig, has taken on a paper route to save enough money for a new color set. But when bank robbers show up and threaten his dreams, and Lisa and Oliver are held captive in their own home, Arnold has to fight back.

programme.title: Flight To Nowhere
programme.desc: The farmers of Hooterville are headed to France! Or at least they are if they can get organized. As part of an agricultural exchange program, the farmers are offered a chance to join a chartered government flight to Europe.

programme.title: My Mother The Countess
programme.desc: Lisa (Eva Gabor) is delighted to learn that her Hungarian mother is planning a visit to their little farm. Much to Oliver's (Eddie Albert) disbelief and dismay, a rumor spreads all around Hooterville that Lisa's mother is a European countess.

programme.title: The Spring Festival
programme.desc: Love is in the air as the first day of spring arrives in Hooterville. And who better to lead the town in celebrating it than Lisa. She is resolved to show Oliver just what spring is all about and sets out to produce a Spring festival celebration.

programme.title: Our Son, The Barber
programme.desc: It's a hair-raising episode as Eb (Tom Lester) enrolls in a correspondence course to become a barber. Lisa (Eva Gabor) and Oliver (Eddie Albert) give him the money to learn the trade but learning how to cut hair by mail has its glitches.

programme.title: Gomez, The Reluctant Lover
programme.desc: Pugsley copies one of his dad's old love letters and sends it to his teacher. Miss Dunbar comes to visit the Addams home and confesses to Gomez that no man has ever fallen so in love with her, leading Morticia to pretend to be the jealous wife.

programme.title: Feud In The Addams Family
programme.desc: After Wednesday befriends a new boy her age, Robespierre Courtney, Morticia invites his parents, Mr. and Mrs. Henry Courtney, to visit the Addams house for tea. Unbeknownst to them, the Courtney's are hoping to meet a number of the social set.

programme.title: Gomez, The Cat Burglar
programme.desc: There is a neighborhood burglar on the loose, and Morticia has reason to believe it could be Gomez. Morticia decides to probe his subconscious mind for a key to his criminality after she and Uncle Fester discover loot hidden in the storeroom.

programme.title: Portrait Of Gomez
programme.desc: Morticia's pet plant Cleopatra gobbles up her favorite photo of Gomez from the family album. When the original photographer is found and asked to make a duplicate picture, it is discovered that he is now with the Department of Motor Vehicles.

programme.title: Morticia's Dilemma
programme.desc: Gomez plays host to Don Xavier Molines, a family friend during his childhood in Spain, and his daughter, Consuella. Morticia's hospitality is put to the test when Consuella reveals that she has been promised in marriage to Gomez since childhood.

programme.title: Christmas With The Addams Family
programme.desc: When Wednesday's and Pugsley's faith in Santa Claus is shaken, Uncle Fester is chosen to back up Santa in case the real St. Nick is too busy to come. However, Fester-Santa gets stuck in the chimney, so each of the other adults fill in for him.

programme.title: Uncle Fester, Tycoon
programme.desc: Fester is so smitten upon receipt of a photo of his love, a bearded carnival lady, that he borrows stamp money to mail her a proposal. Morticia dons a beard and poses as the mother to convince Fester that he will have to work to support a wife.

programme.title: Morticia & Gomez Vs. Fester & Grandmama
programme.desc: Because she thinks Fester (Jackie Coogan) and Grandmama (Blossom Rock) are spoiling the children, Morticia (Carolyn Jones) hires a governess. Insulted, Fester paints a white line down the center of the Addams house, dividing it in half.

programme.title: Fester Goes On A Diet
programme.desc: Morticia and Gomez see Fester conferring with TV muscleman Jack LaGrann (Jack LaLanne), and think that Fester is planning a television career. However, Uncle Fester confides to Lurch that he has to go on a secret diet and exercise.

programme.title: The Great Treasure Hunt
programme.desc: Morticia and Gomez find Grandfather Pegleg's treasure map and summon Captain Grimby. They hope to charter his boat for the treasure hunt. But after meeting the Captain, Morticia is careful to keep the nature of the mission secret.

programme.title: Ophelia Finds Romance
programme.desc: Ophelia Frump is madly in love with Horatio Bartholomew, the man who has everything, except the truth about her relatives. Morticia, not convinced by Bartholomew, persuades playboy Cousin Itt to stir the embers of his past romance with Ophelia.

programme.title: Pugsley's Allowance
programme.desc: Pugsley shocks his parents when he gives up his new atomic reactor as kid stuff and announces that he wants to find his own job. His first try is as a medical assistant to Dr. Bird. He turns up, scalpel in hand, ready to help during an operation.

programme.title: Happy Birthday, Grandma Frump
programme.desc: Grandma Frump (guest star Margaret Hamilton) comes to the mistaken conclusion that her daughter, Morticia (Carolyn Jones), and Gomez (John Astin) plan to pack her off to an old folks home. Actually, they plan to send her to a beauty farm.

programme.title: Morticia, The Decorator
programme.desc: Morticia, in order to be part of the National Beautification Program, decides to become a home decorator and offers her services to the neighbors. Next door neighbor, and insurance agent, Joe Digby hires Morticia in hopes of selling them insurance.

programme.title: Ophelia Visits Morticia
programme.desc: Ophelia taunts Uncle Fester into announcing that he is going to join the Peace Corps. Fester feels he's a cinch to be accepted because Gomez plans to pull strings in Washington on his behalf. The entire family agrees to help him study for the exam.

programme.title: Addams Cum Laude
programme.desc: Allyn Joslyn guest-stars as Sam Hilliard, a former truant officer who now heads a private school where Wednesday and Pugsley are enrolled. When Hilliard breaks under the strain of dealing with the Addams children, Gomez buys the school.

programme.title: La noche más fría
programme.desc: The story focuses on Carlos, a former fighter from the Malvinas (Faulkland islands). Carlos lives in a large city square, under a gazebo that he found in the neighborhood. He tragically lost his family in the War. Times were not kind to Carlos. While looking to reunite with his son, he sweeps sidewalks in exchange for tips to help him survive winter nights which have a punishing effect on Carlo's health. Preparing for the coldest night of the year, driven by the impulse to make it, Carlos packs few belongings to seek refuge in a municipal locality. However, the luck again avoids him. An incident at the door of the shelter leaves Carlos unfairly outside. Completely unprotected, away from his usual spot, without shelter and weak health. The coldest night of the year is looming, and Carlos must face endless adversities in an uneven fight against two cruel enemies: loneliness and cold. Thus begins the coldest night only fueled by the desire to survive.
